indian-knowledge-systems.description: What living models of traditional Hindu education still survive in India — and what do they demonstrate about the alternative to Western schooling? Field studies of surviving gurukulas, pathshalas, and vidyalayas documenting how Indian Knowledge Systems are transmitted in practice today.

indian-knowledge-systems.title: Hindu Response to Enlightenment
indian-knowledge-systems.description: Where does Enlightenment rationalism reveal its own metaphysical blind spots — and what does Hindu philosophy offer in response? A civilizational dialogue contrasting Western secular universalism with Vedantic metaphysical plurality, proposing a Hindu diagnostic rather than a defensive rejection.

indian-knowledge-systems.title: Hindu View of the Simulation Hypothesis
indian-knowledge-systems.description: Nick Bostrom's simulation hypothesis examined alongside the concept of māyā — where the two frameworks converge, where they fundamentally diverge, and why the Hindu model is teleologically richer than the computational one.

indian-knowledge-systems.title: How Old is the Hindu Temple?
indian-knowledge-systems.description: The antiquity of the Hindu temple examined through textual, sculptural, and archaeological evidence — dismantling the Western academic claim that temple architecture was a Buddhist invention absorbed by Hinduism.

indian-knowledge-systems.title: Kapila Vatsyayana
indian-knowledge-systems.description: Six decades of scholarship on the philosophy underlying every Indian art form — dance, architecture, painting, sculpture — Vatsyayana built the most comprehensive modern account of how Hindu arts form a unified system of knowledge, not a collection of disciplines.

indian-knowledge-systems.title: The Many Meanings of the Hindu Temple
indian-knowledge-systems.description: The Hindu temple as simultaneously sacred mountain, cosmic axis, human body, and gateway between worlds — a layered symbolic reading of the temple form as encoded theological knowledge made visible in stone.

indian-knowledge-systems.title: MS Hiriyanna
indian-knowledge-systems.description: The definitive modern scholar of Indian philosophy: his systematic expositions of the darshanas remain the clearest English-language entry point into Hindu metaphysics and epistemology — rigorous without being reductive.

indian-knowledge-systems.title: On What is a Hindu Temple
indian-knowledge-systems.description: Dr. R. Nagaswamy on the essential nature of the Hindu temple — what it actually is, why its origins predate Buddhism, and how the Western academic genealogy that credits Buddhism with inventing the temple form gets the history exactly backward.

indian-knowledge-systems.title: What Do Hindus Practice, but Cannot Prove?
indian-knowledge-systems.description: What do Hindus practice with deep śraddhā but cannot prove — karma phala siddhānta, reincarnation, mantra efficacy — and what is the right epistemic posture toward such axioms?

indian-knowledge-systems.title: Puranas as the Source for a Chronology of Indian History
indian-knowledge-systems.description: A Purāṇic chronological framework for ancient Indian history — building on Pargiter's genealogical reconstruction and Subhash Kak's Mahābhārata dating to propose a coherent timeline for the previous six manvantaras.

indian-knowledge-systems.title: Teaching and Research - The Rot That Has Set In
indian-knowledge-systems.description: The HRD Ministry's proposal to de-emphasize research in college promotions — why the policy response is inadequate to the depth of the rot, and what the real diagnosis of India's academic collapse requires.

indian-knowledge-systems.title: Wade Davis
indian-knowledge-systems.description: An ethnobotanist who spent decades living with indigenous peoples across the world and documented what is actually lost when a language or culture dies — not diversity as abstraction, but irreplaceable knowledge systems encoded over millennia.

indian-knowledge-systems.title: Introduction to IKS
indian-knowledge-systems.author: Kapil Kapoor
indian-knowledge-systems.description: Survey introduction to Indian Knowledge Systems and their major domains, methods, and categories.

indian-knowledge-systems.title: On the Nature of Mathematics and Scientific Knowledge in Indian Tradition
indian-knowledge-systems.author: MD Srinivas
indian-knowledge-systems.description: Study of how Indian traditions theorized mathematics, knowledge, and scientific reasoning.

indian-knowledge-systems.title: Historical Methods
indian-knowledge-systems.author: Shonaleeka Kaul
indian-knowledge-systems.description: Discussion of historical method with relevance to Sanskrit and Indian civilizational sources.

indian-knowledge-systems.title: Indian Science and Technology in the 18th Century
indian-knowledge-systems.author: Dharampal
indian-knowledge-systems.description: Documentary study of scientific and technical practices in early modern India.

indian-knowledge-systems.title: Agnihotra Kranti
indian-knowledge-systems.author: Unknown
indian-knowledge-systems.description: Text advocating or explaining the revival of Agnihotra and related Vedic fire ritual practice.

indian-knowledge-systems.title: Lokopakara
indian-knowledge-systems.author: Unknown
indian-knowledge-systems.description: Traditional practical compendium on useful arts, household knowledge, and applied sciences.

indian-knowledge-systems.title: 64 Arts in Ancient India
indian-knowledge-systems.author: Anil Baran Ganguly
indian-knowledge-systems.description: Survey of the classical list of sixty-four arts and what it reveals about education and cultured accomplishment.

indian-knowledge-systems.title: The Kalās
indian-knowledge-systems.author: A Venkatasubbiah
indian-knowledge-systems.description: Work on Indian Knowledge Systems, bringing traditional categories into conversation with practice, theory, or pedagogy.

indian-knowledge-systems.title: The Beautiful Tree
indian-knowledge-systems.author: Dharampal
indian-knowledge-systems.description: Historical argument about indigenous education in India before colonial disruption.

indian-knowledge-systems.title: Art Experience
indian-knowledge-systems.author: M Hiriyanna
indian-knowledge-systems.description: Reflection on art, meaning, symbolism, and the experience of beauty in Indian thought.

indian-knowledge-systems.title: On Natyashastra
indian-knowledge-systems.author: Bharat Gupt
indian-knowledge-systems.description: Introductory or interpretive study of the Natyashastra and its framework for drama, performance, and rasa.

indian-knowledge-systems.title: Indian Concept of Performance
indian-knowledge-systems.author: Bharat Gupt
indian-knowledge-systems.description: Study of performance as a conceptual and embodied category in Indian aesthetic theory.

karma.title: Civilizations as Kārmika Streams
karma.description: Civilizations are not collections of events but kārmika streams — living ontological entities shaped by accumulated collective action across deep time. An essay that reframes civilizational history as metaphysics.

karma.title: What Do Hindus Practice, but Cannot Prove?
karma.description: What do Hindus practice with deep śraddhā but cannot prove — karma phala siddhānta, reincarnation, mantra efficacy — and what is the right epistemic posture toward such axioms?

karma.title: Katha Upanishad
karma.description: English edition or translation of the Katha Upanishad, highlighting death, the self, disciplined choice, and liberation through insight.

karma.title: Isha Upanishad
karma.description: English edition or translation of the Isha Upanishad, highlighting renunciation, action, inner self, and the relation between knowledge and world-engagement.

karma.title: Bhagavata Purana
karma.description: Edition or translation of the Bhagavata Purana, a major Vaishnava Purana centered on Krishna, bhakti, cosmology, and liberation.

mahabharata.title: Recollection: The Glory of Śrī Kṛṣṇa Janmāṣṭamī
mahabharata.description: A pilgrimage to Mathurā, the city of Kṛṣṇa's birth: how a journey through the geography of the Kṛṣṇa legend becomes a direct encounter with the living presence of the divine in place, available to anyone who knows how to look.

mahabharata.title: Kṣetra, Śāstra, Utsava
mahabharata.description: On the axis connecting festival (utsava), sacred geography (kṣetra), and scripture (śāstra): how Hindu festivals are to time what temples are to space — gradients of divine access that structure both the year and the land.

mahabharata.title: Argument and Design the Unity of the Mahabharata
mahabharata.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
mahabharata.description: Study arguing for the literary and philosophical unity of the Mahabharata against fragmenting critical models.

mahabharata.title: Prolegomena to any Future Mahabharata Studies
mahabharata.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
mahabharata.description: Methodological essay rethinking how the Mahabharata should be studied, edited, and interpreted.

mahabharata.title: Harivansha Purana
mahabharata.description: Edition or translation of the Harivansha Purana, Krishna lineage narratives and cosmological material closely tied to the Mahabharata tradition.

mahabharata.title: Hermeneutics and Narrative Architecture
mahabharata.author: Vishwa Adluri
mahabharata.description: Study of how interpretive method and narrative design work together in Sanskrit epic literature.

modernity.title: 100 Years of Russian Revolution - II - How Communism Killed Freedom of Expression
modernity.description: How the Soviet suppression of free expression is not a historical curiosity but a living template — the Award Wapsi movement in India as a direct inheritance of communist tactics for manufacturing dissent.

modernity.title: 100 Years of Russian Revolution - V - How Marxism Still Influences Indian Politics
modernity.description: Closing the Russian Revolution series: why BJP dominance has not displaced Marxism from India's institutions — the left's grip on academia, judiciary, and media persists beneath the electoral surface.

modernity.title: Artificial Intelligence is Here - Part 1, from Before to Now
modernity.description: AI arrives not as neutral technology but as a civilizational event: a comparison of simulation theory, Vedānta, and modern physics asks whether the intelligence emerging from our machines is something the Dhārmika tradition already has a vocabulary for.

modernity.title: Artificial Intelligence is Here - Part 2, from Now to Henceforth
modernity.description: Part 2: a proactive Dhārmika framework for navigating artificial intelligence — neither uncritical adoption nor reflexive rejection, but a principled engagement grounded in the Indian understanding of consciousness and purpose.

modernity.title: A Dharmika Frame for Sustainability and Technology
modernity.description: The Samudra Manthana as an ancient framework for thinking about technology, resource extraction, and sustainable cooperation — a Dhārmika alternative to both extractivist capitalism and romantic environmentalism.

modernity.title: Equality vs Nature - How Hierarchy Can Help Us Save the Planet
modernity.description: The ecological crisis requires hierarchy, not equality — why the modern fetish of flat-horizontal social organization mirrors and accelerates the environmental destruction it claims to oppose, and what the dharmic alternative looks like.

modernity.title: Grasping for the Beyond - On a Critical Schism in the Modern Psyche
modernity.description: Weber's disenchantment and Jung's diagnosis of the Western mind as a madhouse of abstractions — arguing that the modern psyche's loss of the sacred is precisely the illness that India's living metaphysical tradition can address.

modernity.title: Hindu View of the Simulation Hypothesis
modernity.description: Nick Bostrom's simulation hypothesis examined alongside the concept of māyā — where the two frameworks converge, where they fundamentally diverge, and why the Hindu model is teleologically richer than the computational one.

modernity.title: Loren Eiseley
modernity.description: An anthropologist who wrote like a poet and thought like a mystic — Eiseley's meditations on time, evolution, and consciousness are the closest Western science has come to the Hindu sense of cosmic scale and existential wonder without borrowing the vocabulary.

modernity.title: Is Modernity Compatible With Hindu Values?
modernity.description: Is modernity — with its secularism, rights-framework, and assault on varṇāśrama dharma — compatible with Hindu values, or structurally hostile to Hindu civilization?

modernity.title: Nassim Nicholas Taleb
modernity.description: The philosopher of fragility who argues that modern civilization traded a robust, decentralized wisdom tradition for brittle expert systems — and that this trade is actively destroying us while we congratulate ourselves on progress.

modernity.title: Teaching and Research - The Rot That Has Set In
modernity.description: The HRD Ministry's proposal to de-emphasize research in college promotions — why the policy response is inadequate to the depth of the rot, and what the real diagnosis of India's academic collapse requires.

modernity.title: Theodore Dalrymple
modernity.description: A prison doctor who documented from the inside the cultural wreckage produced by Western progressivism — his clinical observations are the evidence base for what political traditionalists argue at the level of theory.

modernity.title: Thou Shalt Not Comment - The Dictatorship of Scroll.in
modernity.description: How Scroll.in built ideological censorship into its comment moderation — the left-liberal digital media's control of who gets to respond, and why shutting down the comments section was the tell that revealed the site's function as propaganda rather than journalism.

modernity.title: Walking the Tightrope Between Big Brother and Soma
modernity.description: India walks a tightrope between two dystopias — Orwell's totalitarian surveillance state and Huxley's pleasure-addicted passivity. A Dhārmika reading of both futures and the framework that could thread between them.

modernity.title: The Truth and the Humbug in Greta Thunberg's Climate Change Speech
modernity.description: Separating the genuine environmental alarm in Greta Thunberg's speech from its ideological packaging — what the climate movement gets right, what it gets wrong, and why the solution requires hierarchy and dharma rather than equality and activism.

modernity.title: Heidegger and the Inquiry into Technology
modernity.author: Joydeep Bagchee
modernity.description: Essay using Heidegger to interrogate technology, modernity, and civilizational critique.

modernity.title: The Crisis of Hinduism
modernity.author: AK Saran
modernity.description: Reflection on modern challenges facing Hindu civilizational confidence, continuity, and self-understanding.

modernity.title: Gandhis Theory of Society and Our Times
modernity.author: AK Saran
modernity.description: Interpretive study of Gandhi’s social philosophy, moral politics, and continuing relevance.

modernity.title: Essays on Tradition, Recovery and Freedom
modernity.author: Dharampal
modernity.description: Essays on cultural continuity, intellectual decolonization, and the recovery of civilizational freedom.

modernity.title: The Real Threat to Humanities Today
modernity.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
modernity.description: Essay arguing that methodological decay, not external pressure alone, endangers the humanities.

navaratri.title: Brahmacāriṇī | Navadurgā - Part 2
navaratri.description: Second in the Navadurgā series: the theology and iconography of Brahmacāriṇī Devī — the form of Durgā who represents austerity, renunciation, and the discipline through which the soul approaches the absolute.

navaratri.title: Candraghaṇṭā | Navadurgā - Part 3
navaratri.description: Third in the Navadurgā series: the theology and iconography of Candraghaṇṭā Devī — the warrior form whose crescent-bell emblem signals the transition from inner austerity to active, world-facing śakti.

navaratri.title: Kālarātri | Navadurgā - Part 7
navaratri.description: Seventh in the Navadurgā series: the theology and iconography of Kālarātri Devī — the most fearsome form, destroyer of darkness and ignorance, whose terrifying appearance conceals absolute protection for the devotee.

navaratri.title: Kātyāyanī | Navadurgā - Part 6
navaratri.description: Sixth in the Navadurgā series: the theology and iconography of Kātyāyanī Devī — the fierce warrior form born to destroy the demon Mahiṣāsura, and the deity invoked for strength in the face of civilizational threat.

navaratri.title: Kūṣmāṇḍā | Navadurgā - Part 4
navaratri.description: Fourth in the Navadurgā series: the theology and iconography of Kūṣmāṇḍā Devī — the form who created the universe with her smile, and who is propitiated for health, vitality, and the removal of darkness.

navaratri.title: Mahāgaurī | Navadurgā - Part 8
navaratri.description: Eighth in the Navadurgā series: the theology and iconography of Mahāgaurī Devī — the luminous, peaceful form that follows Kālarātri, representing the purity and grace that emerge after the destruction of all that is false.

navaratri.title: Śailaputrī | Navadurgā - Part 1
navaratri.description: Opening the Navadurgā series: an introduction to the Navarātrī tradition followed by the theology and iconography of Śailaputrī Devī — daughter of the mountain, first of the nine forms, embodiment of primal śakti.

navaratri.title: Siddhidātrī | Navadurgā - Part 9
navaratri.description: Ninth and final in the Navadurgā series: the theology and iconography of Siddhidātrī Devī — the granter of all siddhis, the form who completes the Navarātrī cycle and whose worship is the culmination of the entire nine-day journey.

navaratri.title: Skandamātā | Navadurgā - Part 5
navaratri.description: Fifth in the Navadurgā series: the theology and iconography of Skandamātā Devī — mother of Skanda (Kārttikeya), the form that holds together the energies of motherhood, courage, and cosmic order.

northeast-india.title: The Battle for Dharma in North-East
northeast-india.description: What has large-scale conversion and demographic change done to the Dhārmika civilizational fabric of Northeast India? A deep policy study of how missionary activity and political isolation have severed the region from the rest of Bhārata — and what reversal requires.

northeast-india.title: The Battle Against Hindus in Manipur
northeast-india.description: The Manipur conflict is not a tribal dispute but a civilizational one: how the Christian missionary conversion of Kuki tribes created a political wedge that the Indian state misreads as ethnic tension while Meitei Hindu identity is the actual target.

northeast-india.title: Dangs - Anatomy of Religious Terror
northeast-india.description: What does the full anatomy of forced conversion look like in an Indian tribal district — and who bears the cost? A ground-level documentation of missionary operations in Dangs, Gujarat, tracing the cultural and social consequences for the Adivasi communities most affected.

northeast-india.title: A Year in Hajong Village
northeast-india.description: How does a small Hindu tribal community in a Christian-majority region preserve its religious and cultural identity under conversion pressure? An ethnographic documentation of Hajong festivals, rituals, and livelihoods in Meghalaya's Garo Hills — a community invisible to most academic and policy discourse.

out-of-india-theory.title: Bhāratīya Wanderlust - A Defence of the Out-of-India Model (OIT), Part 1
out-of-india-theory.description: Part 1 of a three-part dismantling of the Aryan Invasion/Migration Theory: the textual, genetic, and archaeological evidence increasingly supports an Out-of-India model for Indo-European dispersal — and the implications are civilizationally significant.

out-of-india-theory.title: Bhāratīya Wanderlust - A Defence of the Out-of-India Model (OIT), Part 2
out-of-india-theory.description: Part 2: examining the textual evidence for the Out-of-India model — what the Ṛgveda and comparative philology actually show when read without the Invasion Theory's assumptions baked in from the start.

out-of-india-theory.title: Bhāratīya Wanderlust - A Defence of the Out-of-India Model (OIT), Part 3
out-of-india-theory.description: Part 3: archaeological and genetic evidence for Out-of-India, concluding with a positive reconstruction of what the actual Āryan dispersal looked like and what it means for India's understanding of its civilizational origins.

out-of-india-theory.title: In Memoriam - Life and Works of Shri N.S. Rajaram
out-of-india-theory.description: Obituary and intellectual portrait of N.S. Rajaram — his place in the Voice of India tradition, his contributions to Vedic chronology and the Out of India theory, and what his death means for the Hindu Renaissance.

out-of-india-theory.title: Puranas as the Source for a Chronology of Indian History
out-of-india-theory.description: A Purāṇic chronological framework for ancient Indian history — building on Pargiter's genealogical reconstruction and Subhash Kak's Mahābhārata dating to propose a coherent timeline for the previous six manvantaras.

out-of-india-theory.title: Ratha as a Bīja of Civilizational DNA, a Fractal Maṇḍala Essay
out-of-india-theory.description: The ratha (chariot) as a bīja — a seed form that contains the entire Dhārmika worldview in compressed symbolic code — an essay in reading Hindu iconography as philosophical argument embedded in form.

out-of-india-theory.title: Witzel's Realm - On Reputationist Concerns Over India's Reclamation of Its History
out-of-india-theory.description: A direct response to Michael Witzel's attack on Indian historical revisionism — defending the legitimacy of indigenous Purāṇic chronology against Western academic gatekeeping dressed up as methodological rigor.

out-of-india-theory.title: Rigveda a Historical Analysis
out-of-india-theory.author: Shrikant Talageri
out-of-india-theory.description: Historical reading of the Rigveda that uses textual evidence to discuss chronology, culture, and social setting.

out-of-india-theory.title: Argument and Design the Unity of the Mahabharata
out-of-india-theory.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
out-of-india-theory.description: Study arguing for the literary and philosophical unity of the Mahabharata against fragmenting critical models.

out-of-india-theory.title: Prolegomena to any Future Mahabharata Studies
out-of-india-theory.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
out-of-india-theory.description: Methodological essay rethinking how the Mahabharata should be studied, edited, and interpreted.

out-of-india-theory.title: Geography of the Puranas
out-of-india-theory.author: MA Ali
out-of-india-theory.description: Edition or translation of the Geography of the Puranas, a Purana text combining mythology, cosmology, ritual observance, and sacred geography.

purva-paksha.title: 100 Years of Russian Revolution - I - Is Communism Compatible with Democracy?
purva-paksha.description: Opening the Russian Revolution centenary series: the structural case that communism and democracy are incompatible — not by accident but by design, since the Leninist seizure of power required abolishing democratic legitimacy.

purva-paksha.title: Alexander Solzhenitsyn
purva-paksha.description: The man who survived the Soviet gulag and wrote its testimony: Solzhenitsyn's warning that Western atheist materialism produces the same totalitarian logic as communism remains unrefuted by anyone who has read him carefully.

purva-paksha.title: Arthur Koestler
purva-paksha.description: A Communist turned anti-Communist who witnessed both fascism and Stalinism firsthand — Koestler's intellectual trajectory maps the 20th century's ideological catastrophes and anticipates the civilizational questions that follow when every utopia collapses.

purva-paksha.title: The Artist in Art
purva-paksha.description: What happens when Western art criticism is turned inside out and evaluated through Hindu aesthetics? This project reverses the colonial critical gaze — applying Hindu aesthetic frameworks as the dominant lens to examine Western art's evolution, theory, and unspoken assumptions.

purva-paksha.title: Freedom of Expression
purva-paksha.description: Why clarity about the nature of prophetic monotheism's claim on speech is a prerequisite for any meaningful Hindu engagement with the politics of free expression — the conceptual tools for a discourse that currently lacks them.

purva-paksha.title: Hindu Response to Enlightenment
purva-paksha.description: Where does Enlightenment rationalism reveal its own metaphysical blind spots — and what does Hindu philosophy offer in response? A civilizational dialogue contrasting Western secular universalism with Vedantic metaphysical plurality, proposing a Hindu diagnostic rather than a defensive rejection.

purva-paksha.title: In the Name of Defending Islam - I - Understanding the Muslim Psyche
purva-paksha.description: The 2020 Delhi Riots as a diagnostic event — what they reveal about the structural dynamics of the radical Muslim psyche, the myth of Hindu provocation, and the pattern that has repeated across fourteen centuries of Islamic expansion.

purva-paksha.title: In the Name of Defending Islam - II - How Radical Muslims Play Victim
purva-paksha.description: How radical Islam systematically inverts the aggressor-victim relationship — the global media architecture that transforms Hindu self-defense into Hindu aggression, and why this inversion is structurally necessary to the ideology.

purva-paksha.title: Ivan Illich
purva-paksha.description: The sharpest institutional critic the West produced: his dissections of schooling, medicine, and economic growth argue that modern institutions systematically destroy the autonomous competence they claim to produce — a critique that hits differently once you've read Dharampal.

purva-paksha.title: Letter to a Moderate Muslim
purva-paksha.description: Closing the Unfinished Agendas series: the open letter that argues the moderate Muslim does not exist — that moderation and radical Islamic ideology are structurally incompatible, and the secular Hindu's belief in this figure is a lethal self-deception.

purva-paksha.title: Nassim Nicholas Taleb
purva-paksha.description: The philosopher of fragility who argues that modern civilization traded a robust, decentralized wisdom tradition for brittle expert systems — and that this trade is actively destroying us while we congratulate ourselves on progress.

purva-paksha.title: Ram Swarup
purva-paksha.description: The thinker who explained what the Vedic gods actually are, how polytheism works as a philosophical system, and why the prophetic monotheist critique of Hinduism fundamentally misunderstands what it is attacking — essential reading before any encounter with missionary argument.

purva-paksha.title: Theodore Dalrymple
purva-paksha.description: A prison doctor who documented from the inside the cultural wreckage produced by Western progressivism — his clinical observations are the evidence base for what political traditionalists argue at the level of theory.

purva-paksha.title: Voice of India School of Śatrubodha
purva-paksha.description: The school that insists on Śatrubodha — knowing your adversary clearly — as the precondition for Hindu survival; its critique of prophetic monotheism as structurally incompatible with a pluralist civilization remains the most rigorous analysis in this space.

purva-paksha.title: Witzel's Realm - On Reputationist Concerns Over India's Reclamation of Its History
purva-paksha.description: A direct response to Michael Witzel's attack on Indian historical revisionism — defending the legitimacy of indigenous Purāṇic chronology against Western academic gatekeeping dressed up as methodological rigor.

purva-paksha.title: Cry Hindutva How Rhetoric Trumps Intellect
purva-paksha.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
purva-paksha.description: Critical intervention on contemporary Hindutva rhetoric and the costs of replacing scholarship with slogans.

purva-paksha.title: The Banality of Indology
purva-paksha.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
purva-paksha.description: Sharp critique of routinized assumptions and methodological shallowness in Indological scholarship.

purva-paksha.title: Theses on Indology
purva-paksha.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
purva-paksha.description: Programmatic theses outlining a critique of Indology and an alternative scholarly orientation.

purva-paksha.title: Methods and Context Rethinking Religion
purva-paksha.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
purva-paksha.description: Methodological critique of dominant religious-studies frameworks and their application to India.

purva-paksha.title: Paradigm Lost the Application of the Historical Critical Method to the Bhagavadgita
purva-paksha.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
purva-paksha.description: Critique of applying historical-critical methods reductively to the Bhagavad Gita.

purva-paksha.title: Philosophy and Criticism
purva-paksha.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
purva-paksha.description: Essay on the relation between philosophical interpretation and critical academic method.

purva-paksha.title: The Nay Science
purva-paksha.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
purva-paksha.description: Provocative reflection on scholarship, critique, and the ideological posture of academic knowledge.

purva-paksha.title: The Real Threat to Humanities Today
purva-paksha.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
purva-paksha.description: Essay arguing that methodological decay, not external pressure alone, endangers the humanities.

purva-paksha.title: Hindu Studies in a Secular Academy
purva-paksha.author: Vishwa Adluri
purva-paksha.description: Reflection on the institutional framing of Hindu studies and the limits of secular academic categories.

purva-paksha.title: Hermeneutics and Narrative Architecture
purva-paksha.author: Vishwa Adluri
purva-paksha.description: Study of how interpretive method and narrative design work together in Sanskrit epic literature.

purva-paksha.title: The Perils of Textual Transmission
purva-paksha.author: Vishwa Adluri
purva-paksha.description: Scholarly work on Indian thought and civilization with relevance to the themes signaled by its title.

rama.title: Śrī Rāma Comes Back to Ayodhyā
rama.description: The Ram Janmabhūmi movement as civilizational memory rather than political campaign: how the 500-year struggle for Ayodhyā maps onto the deeper Hindu understanding of sacred geography and the permanence of divine presence in place.

rama.title: Rāma’s Journey – the Avatāra in You, a Fractal Maṇḍala Essay
rama.description: Rāma's exile and return as inner journey: reading the Rāmāyaṇa not as history but as a map of the soul's trajectory — and why Dīpāvalī marks not just a homecoming but a cosmological reorientation of consciousness.

rama.title: राम आयेंगे!
rama.description: A first-person account of the devotional groundswell preceding the 2024 Ayodhyā pratiṣṭhā: what a spontaneous civilizational awakening looks and feels like from the inside — and what it reveals about the living roots of Hindu consciousness.

sanskrit.title: Decolonizing Language
sanskrit.description: How colonialism froze the evolution of North Indian vernaculars by installing FATE languages (Farsi, Arabic, Turkish, English) as prestige languages — and what it would take to let them grow again.

sanskrit.title: Does India Have a National Language?
sanskrit.description: The national language question as a civilizational problem, not a policy one — why Nehru's Hindi gambit failed, what the absence of a unifying language costs India, and what Sanskrit's role should be.

sanskrit.title: Sanskṛta vs. FATE Languages
sanskrit.description: The battle between Saṃskṛta and FATE languages (Farsi, Arabic, Turkish, English) for the cognitive and expressive inheritance of India: why language is not a neutral medium but the primary site of civilizational contestation.

sanskrit.title: A Practical Sanskrit Introductory
sanskrit.author: C Wikner
sanskrit.description: Beginner-oriented introduction to Sanskrit language and basic grammar.

sanskrit.title: Sanskrit Grammar
sanskrit.author: WD Whitney
sanskrit.description: Reference-style overview of Sanskrit grammar, inflection, and syntax.

sanskrit.title: Amarakośaḥ Devanāgarī
sanskrit.author: Amarasiṃha
sanskrit.description: Devanagari text of the Amarakosha for direct study of the classical Sanskrit lexicon.

sanskrit.title: Amarakosha English Translation
sanskrit.author: NG Sardesai
sanskrit.description: English translation of the Amarakosha, the classical Sanskrit lexicon arranged by semantic groupings.

sanskrit.title: Illustrated Amarakosha in 4 Languages
sanskrit.author: Dr. H Madhav
sanskrit.description: Multilingual and illustrated presentation of the Amarakosha for comparative and pedagogical use.

sanskrit.title: The Knowledge Structure in the Amarakosha
sanskrit.author: Sivaja S. Nair
sanskrit.description: Study of how the Amarakosha encodes categories, semantic relations, and systems of knowledge.

sanskrit.title: Vākyapadīya
sanskrit.author: Bhartṛhari
sanskrit.description: Foundational work of Bhartrhari on language, meaning, cognition, and the philosophy of linguistic unity.

sanskrit.title: A Scheme for Knowledge Representation in Samskritam
sanskrit.author: P. Ramanujan
sanskrit.description: Paper exploring Sanskrit-based formal knowledge representation and semantic structuring.

sanskrit.title: Introduction to Sanskrit Shabdamitra
sanskrit.author: Kulkarni, Joshi et al
sanskrit.description: Introductory Sanskrit learning aid focused on vocabulary, forms, and basic usage.

shatrubodha.title: 100 Years of Russian Revolution - I - Is Communism Compatible with Democracy?
shatrubodha.description: Opening the Russian Revolution centenary series: the structural case that communism and democracy are incompatible — not by accident but by design, since the Leninist seizure of power required abolishing democratic legitimacy.

shatrubodha.title: 100 Years of Russian Revolution - II - How Communism Killed Freedom of Expression
shatrubodha.description: How the Soviet suppression of free expression is not a historical curiosity but a living template — the Award Wapsi movement in India as a direct inheritance of communist tactics for manufacturing dissent.

shatrubodha.title: 100 Years of Russian Revolution - III - Communism and Treason
shatrubodha.description: The JNU sedition scandal as a window into communism's structural relationship with treason — why the ideology, from Lenin onward, has always required betraying the nation in service of the global revolution.

shatrubodha.title: 100 Years of Russian Revolution - IV - Was Indira Gandhi a Soviet Agent?
shatrubodha.description: The Mitrokhin Archive and what it reveals about KGB penetration of Indian politics — the evidence that Indira Gandhi's relationship with the Soviet Union went far beyond diplomatic alignment.

shatrubodha.title: 100 Years of Russian Revolution - V - How Marxism Still Influences Indian Politics
shatrubodha.description: Closing the Russian Revolution series: why BJP dominance has not displaced Marxism from India's institutions — the left's grip on academia, judiciary, and media persists beneath the electoral surface.

shatrubodha.title: Azaan and Freedom of Expression - Are They Compatible?
shatrubodha.description: The Sonu Nigam controversy as a test case for a structural question: whether the Islamic claim to unrestricted public religious expression can coexist with the Hindu majority's right to uninterrupted private space.

shatrubodha.title: The Battle for Dharma in North-East
shatrubodha.description: What has large-scale conversion and demographic change done to the Dhārmika civilizational fabric of Northeast India? A deep policy study of how missionary activity and political isolation have severed the region from the rest of Bhārata — and what reversal requires.

shatrubodha.title: The Battle Against Hindus in Manipur
shatrubodha.description: The Manipur conflict is not a tribal dispute but a civilizational one: how the Christian missionary conversion of Kuki tribes created a political wedge that the Indian state misreads as ethnic tension while Meitei Hindu identity is the actual target.

shatrubodha.title: Case Against AOL - A Case of Malicious Prosecution
shatrubodha.description: How Hindu spiritual organizations become targets of malicious prosecution — the Art of Living controversy as a case study in the left-missionary alliance that weaponizes courts and media against Hindu public figures.

shatrubodha.title: Closing the Pamir Gap - The Great Game Series
shatrubodha.description: How the Great Game played out simultaneously across multiple theaters — the closing of the Pamir Gap, Russian annexations from Manchu China, and Britain's maneuvering in East Asia all as connected moves in a single geopolitical contest.

shatrubodha.title: Dangs - Anatomy of Religious Terror
shatrubodha.description: What does the full anatomy of forced conversion look like in an Indian tribal district — and who bears the cost? A ground-level documentation of missionary operations in Dangs, Gujarat, tracing the cultural and social consequences for the Adivasi communities most affected.

shatrubodha.title: The Difference Between Us - on One-life vs. Multiple-life Metaphysics
shatrubodha.description: What actually separates the Hindu and Abrahamic worldviews at the deepest level is not ritual or scripture but the one-life versus multiple-life metaphysical foundation — and the implications of that single difference ramify through everything.

shatrubodha.title: Fighting for India - Britain vs Russia in the Great Game
shatrubodha.description: The Galwan Valley clash traced back to its root cause: the Great Game's dismantling of India's traditional buffer states — why India now borders China at all is a legacy of 19th-century British-Russian competition.

shatrubodha.title: Hindu Liberals - The Slave Soldiers of Islamism
shatrubodha.description: How Hindu liberals function as structural enablers of radical Islam — the Ottoman Devshirme system as a historical analogy for how dominant civilizations conscript their opponents' members to serve as their most effective weapons.

shatrubodha.title: Hindu Samizdat - How Hindus Respond to Censorship and Bias
shatrubodha.description: The emergence of independent Hindu digital media as a samizdat response to mainstream censorship — how the Soviet underground press becomes the analogy for what Hindu journalists built outside the left-controlled media ecosystem.

shatrubodha.title: How Lenin Tried to Foment Communist Revolution in India
shatrubodha.description: The Second Great Game — how Lenin and the early Comintern built networks inside India to foment revolution, continuing the Russian imperial pressure on Britain's Indian possession through ideological rather than military means.

shatrubodha.title: How Scroll.in is Part of Omidyar's Breaking India Network
shatrubodha.description: Scroll.in's financial architecture traced to Omidyar Network — how American philanthropic capital funds the Hinduphobic left media ecosystem in India as part of the broader Breaking India project.

shatrubodha.title: How to Become a Superpower - Inside the Great Game
shatrubodha.description: Central Asia's strategic logic through the lens of Gilgit-Baltistan and the Wakhan Corridor — why the Pamir corridor has always determined superpower reach and why India's loss of these buffer territories still shapes its strategic ceiling.

shatrubodha.title: How Turkey and Germany Planned Holy Jihad Against India
shatrubodha.description: The WWI German-Ottoman plan to use Islamic Jihad to destabilize British India via Persia and Afghanistan — how Enver Pasha and Berlin tried to turn India's Muslim population into a weapon of war.

shatrubodha.title: In the Name of Defending Islam - I - Understanding the Muslim Psyche
shatrubodha.description: The 2020 Delhi Riots as a diagnostic event — what they reveal about the structural dynamics of the radical Muslim psyche, the myth of Hindu provocation, and the pattern that has repeated across fourteen centuries of Islamic expansion.

shatrubodha.title: In the Name of Defending Islam - II - How Radical Muslims Play Victim
shatrubodha.description: How radical Islam systematically inverts the aggressor-victim relationship — the global media architecture that transforms Hindu self-defense into Hindu aggression, and why this inversion is structurally necessary to the ideology.

shatrubodha.title: Intellectual Terrorism of the Left - The Case for Dr. Makarand Paranjape
shatrubodha.description: The cancellation of Makarand Paranjape at Carleton University — how left academic networks suppress Hindu intellectual voices not through argument but through institutional intimidation, and what it reveals about who controls Western universities.

shatrubodha.title: Kaiser, Sultan and the Holy Jihad in India
shatrubodha.description: The Second Great Game — how Germany and Ottoman Turkey built a plan around mobilizing Islamic Jihad inside India during WWI, making the Kaiser and Sultan strange allies in a scheme that came closer to success than most histories acknowledge.

shatrubodha.title: Letter to a Moderate Muslim
shatrubodha.description: Closing the Unfinished Agendas series: the open letter that argues the moderate Muslim does not exist — that moderation and radical Islamic ideology are structurally incompatible, and the secular Hindu's belief in this figure is a lethal self-deception.

shatrubodha.title: Race for Lhasa - The Great Game Series
shatrubodha.description: The race to Tibet at the turn of the 20th century — how Britain and Russia competed for influence over Lhasa after the Pamir Gap was closed, and what the Younghusband Expedition's forced entry into Tibet meant for the final phase of the Great Game.

shatrubodha.title: Ram Swarup
shatrubodha.description: The thinker who explained what the Vedic gods actually are, how polytheism works as a philosophical system, and why the prophetic monotheist critique of Hinduism fundamentally misunderstands what it is attacking — essential reading before any encounter with missionary argument.

shatrubodha.title: Re-imposing the Jizyah - Decoding Sharjeel Imam's Speech
shatrubodha.description: Sharjeel Imam's Shaheen Bagh speech decoded through classical Islamic jurisprudence — the argument that modern India's treatment of Hindus already constitutes an informal Jizyah, and that what Imam demanded was simply its open declaration.

shatrubodha.title: Shri Ram Swarup on the Communist Challenge
shatrubodha.description: Ram Swarup's early writings on communism as a spiritual and civilizational threat — recovering a neglected dimension of the Voice of India thinker whose work on Islam and Christianity overshadowed his equally penetrating critique of Marxism.

shatrubodha.title: Book Review | Svayambodha and Shatrubodha
shatrubodha.description: A review of Pankaj Saxena's *Svayambodha and Shatrubodha*: the book that gives Hindu civilizational discourse its most precise vocabulary for self-knowledge and knowledge of adversarial forces.

shatrubodha.title: Tapan Ghosh - A True Hindu Warrior
shatrubodha.description: Obituary for Tapan Ghosh — the Hindu Samhati founder who gave decades of his life to documenting and resisting the persecution of Hindus in Bengal, and whose death represents an irreplaceable loss to the Bengali Hindu community.

shatrubodha.title: Teaching and Research - The Rot That Has Set In
shatrubodha.description: The HRD Ministry's proposal to de-emphasize research in college promotions — why the policy response is inadequate to the depth of the rot, and what the real diagnosis of India's academic collapse requires.

shatrubodha.title: Thou Shalt Not Comment - The Dictatorship of Scroll.in
shatrubodha.description: How Scroll.in built ideological censorship into its comment moderation — the left-liberal digital media's control of who gets to respond, and why shutting down the comments section was the tell that revealed the site's function as propaganda rather than journalism.

shatrubodha.title: The Truth and the Humbug in Greta Thunberg's Climate Change Speech
shatrubodha.description: Separating the genuine environmental alarm in Greta Thunberg's speech from its ideological packaging — what the climate movement gets right, what it gets wrong, and why the solution requires hierarchy and dharma rather than equality and activism.

shatrubodha.title: Unfinished Agendas of Radical Islam - Case 1 - Lebanon
shatrubodha.description: Lebanon as the case study of how a Muslim minority achieves demographic dominance — tracing the mechanisms by which a Christian-majority country was transformed, and what this trajectory means for every country with a growing Muslim population.

shatrubodha.title: Unfinished Agendas of Radical Islam - Case 2 - Armenia and Turkey
shatrubodha.description: The Armenian Genocide as the first genocide of the 20th century — how Turkey's secular Muslim state proved no less lethal to its Christian minorities than an explicitly Islamic one, and why the denial continues to this day.

shatrubodha.title: Unfinished Agendas of Radical Islam - Case 3 - Greece and Spain
shatrubodha.description: Greece and Spain as the cases of temporarily reversed Islamic conquest — why the population transfers that ended Ottoman rule in Greece are cited by Hindu nationalists as the model India failed to follow, and what Spain's Reconquista reveals about civilizational recovery.

shatrubodha.title: Unfinished Agendas of Radical Islam - The Global Gameplan
shatrubodha.description: Opening the Unfinished Agendas series: the ideological architecture of radical Islam's global strategy — how the anti-CAA riots are not a spontaneous reaction but a calculated move in a long-running civilizational campaign.

shatrubodha.title: Voice of India School of Śatrubodha
shatrubodha.description: The school that insists on Śatrubodha — knowing your adversary clearly — as the precondition for Hindu survival; its critique of prophetic monotheism as structurally incompatible with a pluralist civilization remains the most rigorous analysis in this space.

shatrubodha.title: VS Naipaul
shatrubodha.description: The Nobel laureate who traveled the colonized world and wrote what he actually saw: cultures wrecked by Islam, deformed by colonialism, and unable to recover because they've internalized their conquerors' self-image — a diagnosis as unwelcome now as when he made it.

shatrubodha.title: We the Hindus - What the CAB Means for Hindu Civilization
shatrubodha.description: The Citizenship Amendment Act read as a civilizational turning point — not refugee policy but India's first post-colonial assertion of its Hindu identity, and what the civilizational stakes of this assertion actually are.

shatrubodha.title: What Will Destroy Hindu Society?
shatrubodha.description: Sita Ram Goel's 1981 warning revisited: a rigorous examination of the forces — demographic, cultural, and ideological — most likely to destroy Hindu society.

shatrubodha.title: Why Saba Naqvi is Afraid of Mullahs on TV - And Why We Must Encourage That
shatrubodha.description: The internal contradiction in secular Muslim commentators opposing mullah visibility on TV — what Saba Naqvi's discomfort reveals about who actually speaks for Indian Muslims, and why Hindu society should want the authentic voice to be heard.

shatrubodha.title: What Will Save Sanatana Dharma?
shatrubodha.description: Inspired by the Śaunaka praśna of the Muṇḍaka Upaniṣad: what is the one idea, institution, or action whose presence would ensure the survival of Sanatana Dharma?

shatrubodha.title: Cry Hindutva How Rhetoric Trumps Intellect
shatrubodha.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
shatrubodha.description: Critical intervention on contemporary Hindutva rhetoric and the costs of replacing scholarship with slogans.

shatrubodha.title: German Indology Aryanism and Anti Semitism
shatrubodha.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
shatrubodha.description: Study of German Indology and its entanglement with racial theory, Aryanism, and anti-Semitism.

shatrubodha.title: Greeks in India
shatrubodha.author: Demettios Vasilliades
shatrubodha.description: Historical study of Greek presence in India and the cultural exchanges that followed.

shatrubodha.title: Heidegger and the Inquiry into Technology
shatrubodha.author: Joydeep Bagchee
shatrubodha.description: Essay using Heidegger to interrogate technology, modernity, and civilizational critique.

shatrubodha.title: Hindu Studies in a Secular Academy
shatrubodha.author: Vishwa Adluri
shatrubodha.description: Reflection on the institutional framing of Hindu studies and the limits of secular academic categories.

shatrubodha.title: Hindu Temples Vol 1
shatrubodha.author: Sita Ram Goel
shatrubodha.description: Documentation and interpretation of the destruction, transformation, and historical memory of Hindu temples.

shatrubodha.title: Hindu Temples Vol 2
shatrubodha.author: Sita Ram Goel
shatrubodha.description: Continuation of the documentary record on Hindu temples, iconoclasm, and historical memory.

shatrubodha.title: How I Became a Hindu
shatrubodha.author: Sita Ram Goel
shatrubodha.description: Personal and polemical reflection on religious identity, conviction, and civilizational self-understanding.

shatrubodha.title: Jews and Hindus in Indology
shatrubodha.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
shatrubodha.description: Comparative study of how Jewish and Hindu traditions have been handled within modern Indology.

shatrubodha.title: Methods and Context Rethinking Religion
shatrubodha.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
shatrubodha.description: Methodological critique of dominant religious-studies frameworks and their application to India.

shatrubodha.title: Paradigm Lost the Application of the Historical Critical Method to the Bhagavadgita
shatrubodha.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
shatrubodha.description: Critique of applying historical-critical methods reductively to the Bhagavad Gita.

shatrubodha.title: Philosophy and Criticism
shatrubodha.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
shatrubodha.description: Essay on the relation between philosophical interpretation and critical academic method.

shatrubodha.title: Poetic Immortality to Salvation
shatrubodha.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
shatrubodha.description: Study tracing shifts from heroic-poetic ideals toward soteriological concerns in Sanskrit traditions.

shatrubodha.title: Pride and Prejudice Orientalism and Germany
shatrubodha.author: Vishwa Adluri
shatrubodha.description: Study of German orientalism and the ideological lenses shaping Indological interpretation.

shatrubodha.title: The Banality of Indology
shatrubodha.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
shatrubodha.description: Sharp critique of routinized assumptions and methodological shallowness in Indological scholarship.

shatrubodha.title: The Nay Science
shatrubodha.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
shatrubodha.description: Provocative reflection on scholarship, critique, and the ideological posture of academic knowledge.

shatrubodha.title: The Origins of Racism in the Humanities
shatrubodha.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
shatrubodha.description: Argument about how racial categories entered humanistic scholarship through philology and related disciplines.

shatrubodha.title: The Passion of Paul Hacker
shatrubodha.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
shatrubodha.description: Critical study of Paul Hacker and the theological-political assumptions behind his work on Hindu traditions.

shatrubodha.title: The Real Threat to Humanities Today
shatrubodha.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
shatrubodha.description: Essay arguing that methodological decay, not external pressure alone, endangers the humanities.

shatrubodha.title: Theses on Indology
shatrubodha.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
shatrubodha.description: Programmatic theses outlining a critique of Indology and an alternative scholarly orientation.

svayambodha.title: AK Saran
svayambodha.description: A rare Indian sociologist who refused to think inside British intellectual frameworks — his is one of the few attempts to develop a genuinely Hindu social science, reading the tradition from within rather than against Western categories.

svayambodha.title: What does 'Being Indian' Mean?
svayambodha.description: What does Indian identity mean in light of Partition, the Muslim question, and conflicting nationalist visions from Gandhi to Savarkar to Nehru?

svayambodha.title: Brahmacāriṇī | Navadurgā - Part 2
svayambodha.description: Second in the Navadurgā series: the theology and iconography of Brahmacāriṇī Devī — the form of Durgā who represents austerity, renunciation, and the discipline through which the soul approaches the absolute.

svayambodha.title: Candraghaṇṭā | Navadurgā - Part 3
svayambodha.description: Third in the Navadurgā series: the theology and iconography of Candraghaṇṭā Devī — the warrior form whose crescent-bell emblem signals the transition from inner austerity to active, world-facing śakti.

svayambodha.title: Continuous, Comprehensive and Cumulative - The Knowledge Tradition of India
svayambodha.description: India's knowledge tradition characterized by three properties that colonial historiography denied it — its continuity across millennia, its comprehensiveness across all domains, and its cumulative rather than rupture-based development.

svayambodha.title: Dharampal School of Svayambodha
svayambodha.description: Built on Dharampal's archival discoveries about pre-colonial India, this school argues that Svayambodha — self-knowledge grounded in India's actual historical capacities — is the precondition for any viable Indian future, not a romantic luxury.

svayambodha.title: The Difference Between Us - on One-life vs. Multiple-life Metaphysics
svayambodha.description: What actually separates the Hindu and Abrahamic worldviews at the deepest level is not ritual or scripture but the one-life versus multiple-life metaphysical foundation — and the implications of that single difference ramify through everything.

svayambodha.title: Grasping for the Beyond - On a Critical Schism in the Modern Psyche
svayambodha.description: Weber's disenchantment and Jung's diagnosis of the Western mind as a madhouse of abstractions — arguing that the modern psyche's loss of the sacred is precisely the illness that India's living metaphysical tradition can address.

svayambodha.title: Ivan Illich
svayambodha.description: The sharpest institutional critic the West produced: his dissections of schooling, medicine, and economic growth argue that modern institutions systematically destroy the autonomous competence they claim to produce — a critique that hits differently once you've read Dharampal.

svayambodha.title: Kālarātri | Navadurgā - Part 7
svayambodha.description: Seventh in the Navadurgā series: the theology and iconography of Kālarātri Devī — the most fearsome form, destroyer of darkness and ignorance, whose terrifying appearance conceals absolute protection for the devotee.

svayambodha.title: Kātyāyanī | Navadurgā - Part 6
svayambodha.description: Sixth in the Navadurgā series: the theology and iconography of Kātyāyanī Devī — the fierce warrior form born to destroy the demon Mahiṣāsura, and the deity invoked for strength in the face of civilizational threat.

svayambodha.title: Kūṣmāṇḍā | Navadurgā - Part 4
svayambodha.description: Fourth in the Navadurgā series: the theology and iconography of Kūṣmāṇḍā Devī — the form who created the universe with her smile, and who is propitiated for health, vitality, and the removal of darkness.

svayambodha.title: Mahāgaurī | Navadurgā - Part 8
svayambodha.description: Eighth in the Navadurgā series: the theology and iconography of Mahāgaurī Devī — the luminous, peaceful form that follows Kālarātri, representing the purity and grace that emerge after the destruction of all that is false.

svayambodha.title: Śailaputrī | Navadurgā - Part 1
svayambodha.description: Opening the Navadurgā series: an introduction to the Navarātrī tradition followed by the theology and iconography of Śailaputrī Devī — daughter of the mountain, first of the nine forms, embodiment of primal śakti.

svayambodha.title: Siddhidātrī | Navadurgā - Part 9
svayambodha.description: Ninth and final in the Navadurgā series: the theology and iconography of Siddhidātrī Devī — the granter of all siddhis, the form who completes the Navarātrī cycle and whose worship is the culmination of the entire nine-day journey.

svayambodha.title: Skandamātā | Navadurgā - Part 5
svayambodha.description: Fifth in the Navadurgā series: the theology and iconography of Skandamātā Devī — mother of Skanda (Kārttikeya), the form that holds together the energies of motherhood, courage, and cosmic order.

svayambodha.title: Rigveda a Historical Analysis
svayambodha.author: Shrikant Talageri
svayambodha.description: Historical reading of the Rigveda that uses textual evidence to discuss chronology, culture, and social setting.

svayambodha.title: Rediscovering India
svayambodha.author: Dharampal
svayambodha.description: Essays aimed at recovering Indian civilizational categories, intellectual memory, and cultural self-knowledge.

svayambodha.title: The Beautiful Tree
svayambodha.author: Dharampal
svayambodha.description: Historical argument about indigenous education in India before colonial disruption.

svayambodha.title: Essays on Tradition, Recovery and Freedom
svayambodha.author: Dharampal
svayambodha.description: Essays on cultural continuity, intellectual decolonization, and the recovery of civilizational freedom.

svayambodha.title: Panchayat Raj and India's Polity
svayambodha.author: Dharampal
svayambodha.description: Study of village governance and decentralized political structures in Indian constitutional and civilizational context.

svayambodha.title: Essential Coomaraswamy
svayambodha.author: Ananda Coomaraswamy
svayambodha.description: General overview of Hindu tradition, covering its beliefs, practices, social vision, and intellectual foundations.

svayambodha.title: The Secret of the Veda
svayambodha.author: Sri Aurobindo
svayambodha.description: Sri Aurobindo’s symbolic interpretation of the Vedic hymns as spiritual and psychological texts rather than merely ritual documents.

svayambodha.title: Wisdom of the Ancient Seers
svayambodha.author: David Frawley
svayambodha.description: Introductory reflections on Vedic and Upanishadic insight framed for modern readers.

svayambodha.title: Introduction to IKS
svayambodha.author: Kapil Kapoor
svayambodha.description: Survey introduction to Indian Knowledge Systems and their major domains, methods, and categories.

svayambodha.title: Future of Mankind
svayambodha.author: Sri Aurobindo
svayambodha.description: Civilizational reflection arguing for a spiritually grounded future rooted in perennial wisdom.

temple.title: Ancient Temples and Modern Dacoits Part III - The Architectural Workshop of the Gurjara-Pratiharas
temple.description: The Gurjara-Pratihara dynasty as the defining force in north Indian temple architecture — the temple clusters of Morena and Naresar as laboratories where the distinctive regional style was forged.

temple.title: Dharmic Circuits - Sacred Pathways - Part 1
temple.description: The Hindu geography of Bharatvarsha as a structured sacred space — how teertha yatra creates a living network of centers and pathways that defines the cultural boundaries of the civilization.

temple.title: Dharmic Circuits - The Temple, The Grove and The Lake - Part 3
temple.description: Sringeri as the model of a complete sacred ecology — where the temple, the forest, the river, and the Vedic institution are not separate entities but one inseparable living system.

temple.title: Hindu Models of Temple Management
temple.description: How did the traditional Hindu temple function as a self-governing civilizational institution — and what would it take to recover that from state-imposed bureaucratic management? Research building the case for culture-sensitive temple governance by documenting indigenous models of administration.

temple.title: How Old is the Hindu Temple?
temple.description: The antiquity of the Hindu temple examined through textual, sculptural, and archaeological evidence — dismantling the Western academic claim that temple architecture was a Buddhist invention absorbed by Hinduism.

temple.title: My Journey with Anveshi
temple.description: The origin story of Anveshi, Bodha's structured temple-visit practice: how collective temple-going became a form of civilizational reconnection — and what it reveals about how living tradition actually perpetuates itself.

temple.title: The Many Meanings of the Hindu Temple
temple.description: The Hindu temple as simultaneously sacred mountain, cosmic axis, human body, and gateway between worlds — a layered symbolic reading of the temple form as encoded theological knowledge made visible in stone.

temple.title: On What is a Hindu Temple
temple.description: Dr. R. Nagaswamy on the essential nature of the Hindu temple — what it actually is, why its origins predate Buddhism, and how the Western academic genealogy that credits Buddhism with inventing the temple form gets the history exactly backward.

temple.title: SK Ramachandra Rao
temple.description: A Karnataka polymath whose encyclopedic work on Hindu iconography and temple architecture reveals the metaphysical logic embedded in every sculptural form and spatial arrangement — the temple not as monument but as cosmological diagram made stone.

temple.title: Story of a Priest
temple.description: Portrait of the head priest of the Hoysala-era Veera Narayana Temple at Belavadi — how a 12th-century heritage temple survives and what the priest's life reveals about the community of practice that keeps the living tradition alive.

temple.title: Temples of Madhya Pradesh
temple.description: What do three hundred surviving ancient temple sites of Madhya Pradesh reveal about the full range of Hindu sacred architecture — and what was lost to medieval destruction? A deep architectural and sculptural study of ten major temple clusters, making the grammar of Hindu temple-building legible.

temple.title: Hindu Temples Vol 1
temple.author: Sita Ram Goel
temple.description: Documentation and interpretation of the destruction, transformation, and historical memory of Hindu temples.

temple.title: Hindu Temples Vol 2
temple.author: Sita Ram Goel
temple.description: Continuation of the documentary record on Hindu temples, iconoclasm, and historical memory.

temple.title: Matsya Purana
temple.description: Edition or translation of the Matsya Purana, the flood myth together with temple, iconographic, genealogical, and pilgrimage material.

temple.title: Skanda Purana
temple.description: Edition or translation of the Skanda Purana, a vast pilgrimage-oriented Purana organized around sacred geography and regional mahatmyas.

temple.title: Agni Purana
temple.description: Edition or translation of the Agni Purana, an encyclopedic Purana covering cosmology, ritual, iconography, kingship, and sacred practice.

thinkers.title: School of Conscilience
thinkers.description: This school holds that the West's divorce between sciences and humanities is a civilizational pathology — and that the Indian knowledge tradition, which never separated the two, offers a working model of their reintegration.

thinkers.title: In Memoriam - Life and Works of Shri N.S. Rajaram
thinkers.description: Obituary and intellectual portrait of N.S. Rajaram — his place in the Voice of India tradition, his contributions to Vedic chronology and the Out of India theory, and what his death means for the Hindu Renaissance.

thinkers.title: Intellectual Terrorism of the Left - The Case for Dr. Makarand Paranjape
thinkers.description: The cancellation of Makarand Paranjape at Carleton University — how left academic networks suppress Hindu intellectual voices not through argument but through institutional intimidation, and what it reveals about who controls Western universities.

thinkers.title: Making of Early Kashmir - The Making of a New Literary Star
thinkers.description: Review of Shonaleeka Kaul's The Making of Early Kashmir — how the Rajatarangini constructs Kashmiri identity through sacred landscape, and why this emic reading of history challenges the colonial separation of geography from meaning.

thinkers.title: Shri Ram Swarup on the Communist Challenge
thinkers.description: Ram Swarup's early writings on communism as a spiritual and civilizational threat — recovering a neglected dimension of the Voice of India thinker whose work on Islam and Christianity overshadowed his equally penetrating critique of Marxism.

thinkers.title: Tapan Ghosh - A True Hindu Warrior
thinkers.description: Obituary for Tapan Ghosh — the Hindu Samhati founder who gave decades of his life to documenting and resisting the persecution of Hindus in Bengal, and whose death represents an irreplaceable loss to the Bengali Hindu community.

thinkers.title: Abhinavagupta a Historical and Philosophical Study
thinkers.author: Unknown
thinkers.description: Study of Abhinavagupta’s life, thought, Kashmir Shaivism, and aesthetic philosophy.

thinkers.title: The Passion of Paul Hacker
thinkers.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
thinkers.description: Critical study of Paul Hacker and the theological-political assumptions behind his work on Hindu traditions.

thinkers.title: Essential Coomaraswamy
thinkers.author: Ananda Coomaraswamy
thinkers.description: General overview of Hindu tradition, covering its beliefs, practices, social vision, and intellectual foundations.

thinkers.title: Future of Mankind
thinkers.author: Sri Aurobindo
thinkers.description: Civilizational reflection arguing for a spiritually grounded future rooted in perennial wisdom.

thinkers.title: Essays on Tradition, Recovery and Freedom
thinkers.author: Dharampal
thinkers.description: Essays on cultural continuity, intellectual decolonization, and the recovery of civilizational freedom.

thinkers.title: The Crisis of Hinduism
thinkers.author: AK Saran
thinkers.description: Reflection on modern challenges facing Hindu civilizational confidence, continuity, and self-understanding.

tradition.title: Chaṭh - a Living Tradition and Cultural Homecoming
tradition.description: Chaṭh as lived civilizational memory: a personal account of returning to Bihar for the festival, and what the survival of this demanding, water-centered rite reveals about the deep roots of Hindu devotional practice in the body and the land.

tradition.title: Dharmic Circuits - Sacred Pathways - Part 1
tradition.description: The Hindu geography of Bharatvarsha as a structured sacred space — how teertha yatra creates a living network of centers and pathways that defines the cultural boundaries of the civilization.

tradition.title: Dharmic Circuits - The Temple, The Grove and The Lake - Part 3
tradition.description: Sringeri as the model of a complete sacred ecology — where the temple, the forest, the river, and the Vedic institution are not separate entities but one inseparable living system.

tradition.title: Dukh, Depression and Journeys of Life - Depression is the Absence of Dukkha - Part I
tradition.description: The provocative argument that depression is the absence of duḥkha, not its presence — how the Sanskrit framework for suffering provides an experiential map that modern psychology, focused on symptom-removal, cannot offer.

tradition.title: Kṣetra, Śāstra, Utsava
tradition.description: On the axis connecting festival (utsava), sacred geography (kṣetra), and scripture (śāstra): how Hindu festivals are to time what temples are to space — gradients of divine access that structure both the year and the land.

tradition.title: The Problem of Culture Transmission
tradition.description: Tradition is not the past preserved but a living chain of transmission — an examination of the specific mechanisms by which Hindu civilization has carried its deepest knowledge forward, and where those mechanisms are breaking today.

tradition.title: Story of a Priest
tradition.description: Portrait of the head priest of the Hoysala-era Veera Narayana Temple at Belavadi — how a 12th-century heritage temple survives and what the priest's life reveals about the community of practice that keeps the living tradition alive.

tradition.title: Hinduism Ancient and Modern
tradition.author: Rai Bahadur Lala Baij Nath
tradition.description: Comparative overview of continuities and changes in Hindu tradition across historical periods.

tradition.title: All About Hinduism
tradition.author: Sri Swami Sivananda
tradition.description: Popular overview of Hindu beliefs, practices, festivals, symbols, and philosophical foundations.

tradition.title: Religious Sects of Hindus
tradition.author: HH Wilson
tradition.description: Survey of major Hindu sects, their doctrines, institutions, and devotional lineages.

tradition.title: History of Dharmashastras Vol 1
tradition.author: PV Kane
tradition.description: Reference study of dharmashastra literature, sources of dharma, and the historical formation of legal-ritual norms.

tradition.title: History of Dharmashastras Vol 2 Part 1
tradition.author: PV Kane
tradition.description: Reference study of dharmashastra literature with focus on ritual, samskara, and normative practice.

yoga.title: Swami Vivekananda's School
yoga.description: The school that launched Hindu renaissance: Vivekananda's reading of Vedanta as both universal philosophy and basis for Hindu action produced the template every subsequent Hindu revivalist movement has worked with, consciously or not.

yoga.title: Gheranda Samhita
yoga.author: Translated by Srisa Chandra Vasu
yoga.description: Hatha yoga manual on bodily discipline, purification, mudra, meditation, and yogic accomplishment.

yoga.title: Patanjali's Yogasutras
yoga.author: Patanjali
yoga.description: Edition or translation of the Yoga Sutras with emphasis on mind, practice, samadhi, and liberation.

yoga.title: The Yogasutras of Patanjali
yoga.author: Patanjali, translated by Sriram Lele
yoga.description: Translation and explanation of Patanjali’s Yoga Sutras focused on discipline, concentration, and liberation.

yoga.title: Yogasutra with Commentary of Madhavacharya
yoga.author: Madhvacharya
yoga.description: Yoga Sutra text accompanied by traditional commentary for philosophical and practical interpretation.

itihasa.title: History is Ontic but Itihāsa is Ontologic – a Fractal Maṇḍala Essay
itihasa.description: The distinction between history (ontic, event-centered) and itihāsa (ontologic, truth-centered) is not a quaint traditional category but a methodological difference with profound consequences for how India should understand and narrate its own past.

itihasa.title: Puranas as the Source for a Chronology of Indian History
itihasa.description: A Purāṇic chronological framework for ancient Indian history — building on Pargiter's genealogical reconstruction and Subhash Kak's Mahābhārata dating to propose a coherent timeline for the previous six manvantaras.

itihasa.title: Dāśarājña Recontextualized - Part 1
itihasa.description: Part 1 of a three-part reconstruction of the Dāśarājña (Battle of Ten Kings) as the earliest datable event in Indian history, reexamining Ṛgveda Maṇḍala 7 using Out-of-India chronological frameworks.

itihasa.title: Dāśarājña Recontextualized - Part 2
itihasa.description: Part 2: a close reading of the textual data in Ṛgveda Maṇḍala 7, parsing the battle's participants, geography, and historical context against established chronologies of Indian lineages.

itihasa.title: Dāśarājña Recontextualized - Part 3
itihasa.description: Part 3: the concluding reconstruction of the Dāśarājña — placing the battle in specific historical and geographic context and drawing out its implications for India's deep civilizational memory.

itihasa.title: Bhāratīya Wanderlust - A Defence of the Out-of-India Model (OIT), Part 1
itihasa.description: Part 1 of a three-part dismantling of the Aryan Invasion/Migration Theory: the textual, genetic, and archaeological evidence increasingly supports an Out-of-India model for Indo-European dispersal — and the implications are civilizationally significant.

itihasa.title: Bhāratīya Wanderlust - A Defence of the Out-of-India Model (OIT), Part 2
itihasa.description: Part 2: examining the textual evidence for the Out-of-India model — what the Ṛgveda and comparative philology actually show when read without the Invasion Theory's assumptions baked in from the start.

itihasa.title: Bhāratīya Wanderlust - A Defence of the Out-of-India Model (OIT), Part 3
itihasa.description: Part 3: archaeological and genetic evidence for Out-of-India, concluding with a positive reconstruction of what the actual Āryan dispersal looked like and what it means for India's understanding of its civilizational origins.

itihasa.title: Rāma’s Journey – the Avatāra in You, a Fractal Maṇḍala Essay
itihasa.description: Rāma's exile and return as inner journey: reading the Rāmāyaṇa not as history but as a map of the soul's trajectory — and why Dīpāvalī marks not just a homecoming but a cosmological reorientation of consciousness.

itihasa.title: Fractal Maṇḍala 1- the Macrohistoric Case for India's Civilizational Primacy
itihasa.description: Part 1: the Fractal Maṇḍala as a model of Indian civilizational consciousness — multi-level, coherent, self-similar at every scale — and why recovering this model is the prerequisite for any meaningful cultural or institutional regeneration.

itihasa.title: Fractal Maṇḍala 2- the Macrohistoric Case for India's Civilizational Primacy
itihasa.description: Part 2: completing the Fractal Maṇḍala framework and its implications for how Hindu civilization organizes knowledge, culture, institution, and cosmos into a single coherent ontological structure.

itihasa.title: Grand History, Part 1
itihasa.description: A speculative but evidence-grounded attempt at macro-history: tracing the deep trajectory of human consciousness and civilization from pre-linguistic origins through the emergence of the great civilizational streams.

itihasa.title: Agni Purana
itihasa.description: Edition or translation of the Agni Purana, an encyclopedic Purana covering cosmology, ritual, iconography, kingship, and sacred practice.

itihasa.title: Bhagavata Purana
itihasa.description: Edition or translation of the Bhagavata Purana, a major Vaishnava Purana centered on Krishna, bhakti, cosmology, and liberation.

itihasa.title: Brahma Purana
itihasa.description: Edition or translation of the Brahma Purana, creation narratives, sacred geography, pilgrimage, ritual life, and dynastic lore.

itihasa.title: Brahmanda Purana
itihasa.description: Edition or translation of the Brahmanda Purana, cosmology, cycles of time, genealogies, and later devotional theology.

itihasa.title: Garuda Purana
itihasa.description: Edition or translation of the Garuda Purana, afterlife teachings, funerary rites, ethics, cosmology, and Vaishnava doctrine.

itihasa.title: Geography of the Puranas
itihasa.author: MA Ali
itihasa.description: Edition or translation of the Geography of the Puranas, a Purana text combining mythology, cosmology, ritual observance, and sacred geography.

itihasa.title: Harivansha Purana
itihasa.description: Edition or translation of the Harivansha Purana, Krishna lineage narratives and cosmological material closely tied to the Mahabharata tradition.

itihasa.title: Kurma Purana
itihasa.description: Edition or translation of the Kurma Purana, cosmology, pilgrimage, dharma, and sectarian theology in dialogic form.

itihasa.title: Linga Purana
itihasa.description: Edition or translation of the Linga Purana, Shaiva theology of the linga alongside cosmology, vows, and sacred geography.

itihasa.title: Markandeya Purana
itihasa.description: Edition or translation of the Markandeya Purana, mythic and ethical narrative best known for the Devi Mahatmya and broader cosmological material.

itihasa.title: Matsya Purana
itihasa.description: Edition or translation of the Matsya Purana, the flood myth together with temple, iconographic, genealogical, and pilgrimage material.

itihasa.title: Narada Purana
itihasa.description: Edition or translation of the Narada Purana, ritual, devotion, pilgrimage, and religious observance in a Purana compendium format.

itihasa.title: Padma Purana
itihasa.description: Edition or translation of the Padma Purana, creation, pilgrimage, dharma, sacred places, and large Vaishnava devotional sections.

itihasa.title: Shiva Purana
itihasa.description: Edition or translation of the Shiva Purana, myths, worship, cosmology, and theology centered on Shiva.

itihasa.title: Skanda Purana
itihasa.description: Edition or translation of the Skanda Purana, a vast pilgrimage-oriented Purana organized around sacred geography and regional mahatmyas.

itihasa.title: Vamana Purana
itihasa.description: Edition or translation of the Vamana Purana, mythic narrative, pilgrimage, and Vaishnava themes around the Vamana tradition.

itihasa.title: Varaha Purana
itihasa.description: Edition or translation of the Varaha Purana, Vaishnava cosmology, ritual observance, pilgrimage, and the Varaha incarnation.

itihasa.title: Vayu Purana
itihasa.description: Edition or translation of the Vayu Purana, cosmology, genealogies, dynastic memory, and ancient sacred lore.

itihasa.title: Vishnu Purana
itihasa.description: Edition or translation of the Vishnu Purana, creation, dynasties, avatara doctrine, cosmology, and dharma in a foundational Vaishnava Purana.

itihasa.title: Argument and Design the Unity of the Mahabharata
itihasa.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
itihasa.description: Study arguing for the literary and philosophical unity of the Mahabharata against fragmenting critical models.

itihasa.title: Katha Sarit Sagar
itihasa.author: C.H. Tawney
itihasa.description: Large compendium of narrative literature preserving story cycles, moral reflection, and courtly imagination.

itihasa.title: Prolegomena to any Future Mahabharata Studies
itihasa.author: Vishwa Adluri, Joydeep Bagchee
itihasa.description: Methodological essay rethinking how the Mahabharata should be studied, edited, and interpreted.

metaphysics.title: Art and Meaning Making
metaphysics.description: Hindu aesthetics begins where Western aesthetics ends — the Indian tradition was interested in the effect of art on the soul, not the representation of reality, and this difference reveals two fundamentally incompatible metaphysical starting points.

metaphysics.title: Civilizations as Kārmika Streams
metaphysics.description: Civilizations are not collections of events but kārmika streams — living ontological entities shaped by accumulated collective action across deep time. An essay that reframes civilizational history as metaphysics.

metaphysics.title: The Difference Between Us - on One-life vs. Multiple-life Metaphysics
metaphysics.description: What actually separates the Hindu and Abrahamic worldviews at the deepest level is not ritual or scripture but the one-life versus multiple-life metaphysical foundation — and the implications of that single difference ramify through everything.

metaphysics.title: Fractal Maṇḍala 2- the Macrohistoric Case for India's Civilizational Primacy
