metaphysics.description: Part 2: completing the Fractal Maṇḍala framework and its implications for how Hindu civilization organizes knowledge, culture, institution, and cosmos into a single coherent ontological structure.

metaphysics.title: MS Hiriyanna
metaphysics.description: The definitive modern scholar of Indian philosophy: his systematic expositions of the darshanas remain the clearest English-language entry point into Hindu metaphysics and epistemology — rigorous without being reductive.

metaphysics.title: SK Ramachandra Rao
metaphysics.description: A Karnataka polymath whose encyclopedic work on Hindu iconography and temple architecture reveals the metaphysical logic embedded in every sculptural form and spatial arrangement — the temple not as monument but as cosmological diagram made stone.

metaphysics.title: Preliminary Schema for Synaptic Reconnection to Civilizational Consciousness - Part 1
metaphysics.description: Part 1: a preliminary schema for reconnecting with civilizational consciousness — what it means to fill the empty space that decolonization creates with a genuinely Dhārmika ontology, epistemology, and teleology.

metaphysics.title: Hindu Response to Enlightenment
metaphysics.description: Where does Enlightenment rationalism reveal its own metaphysical blind spots — and what does Hindu philosophy offer in response? A civilizational dialogue contrasting Western secular universalism with Vedantic metaphysical plurality, proposing a Hindu diagnostic rather than a defensive rejection.

metaphysics.title: School of Cultural Traditionalists
metaphysics.description: The oldest living school in India: scholars who see themselves as contemporary interpreters of the same tradition that produced the Upanishads and the Nāṭyaśāstra — treating metaphysics, aesthetics, and philosophy as one continuous project rather than separate departments.

metaphysics.title: Grasping for the Beyond - On a Critical Schism in the Modern Psyche
metaphysics.description: Weber's disenchantment and Jung's diagnosis of the Western mind as a madhouse of abstractions — arguing that the modern psyche's loss of the sacred is precisely the illness that India's living metaphysical tradition can address.

metaphysics.title: Hindu View of the Simulation Hypothesis
metaphysics.description: Nick Bostrom's simulation hypothesis examined alongside the concept of māyā — where the two frameworks converge, where they fundamentally diverge, and why the Hindu model is teleologically richer than the computational one.

metaphysics.title: Caturasūtra - Four Aphorisms
metaphysics.description: Four foundational aphorisms for understanding Indian civilizational consciousness, drawn from across the tradition — compact enough to internalize, substantial enough to orient an entire intellectual project.

metaphysics.title: Logic, Language and Reality
metaphysics.author: Bimal Krishna Matilal
metaphysics.description: Philosophical study connecting logic, linguistic analysis, and theories of reality in Indian thought.

metaphysics.title: The Advaita Vedanta of Brahmasiddhi
metaphysics.author: Allen Wright Thrasher
metaphysics.description: Study of the Brahmasiddhi tradition and major issues in Advaita metaphysics and epistemology.

metaphysics.title: The Paramarthasara of Adisesa
metaphysics.author: Adi Sesa
metaphysics.description: Philosophical text associated with non-dual Shaiva traditions and the path to liberation.

sanatana-dharma.title: What is the Core of Sanatana Dharma?
sanatana-dharma.description: What is the unchanging core of Sanatana Dharma — an idea, an institution, a worldview, or a value system? Bodha's most foundational research question.

sanatana-dharma.title: What Will Save Sanatana Dharma?
sanatana-dharma.description: Inspired by the Śaunaka praśna of the Muṇḍaka Upaniṣad: what is the one idea, institution, or action whose presence would ensure the survival of Sanatana Dharma?

sanatana-dharma.title: Bodha, the Pyramid of Priorities and the Nature of Hindu Organizations
sanatana-dharma.description: Why Hindu organizational fragmentation is a feature, not a failure: Bodha's argument that Sanatana Dharma requires a diversity of independent organizations rather than monolithic unity, and what this means for civilizational strategy.

sanatana-dharma.title: Svayambodha and Śatrubodha - Two Wheels of Civilization
sanatana-dharma.description: Hindu civilizational discourse has entered its sophisticated phase — an argument that Svayambodha (self-knowledge) and Shatrubodha (knowledge of the adversary) now constitute a canonical framework, not merely a trend.

sanatana-dharma.title: Caturasūtra - Four Aphorisms
sanatana-dharma.description: Four foundational aphorisms for understanding Indian civilizational consciousness, drawn from across the tradition — compact enough to internalize, substantial enough to orient an entire intellectual project.

sanatana-dharma.title: The Dhārmika Gene
sanatana-dharma.description: A fundamental re-articulation of Dharma: not as rule or duty but as a civilizational inheritance — the genetic-level encoding that tells Bhāratīya culture how to understand itself, its cosmos, and its obligations across generations.
